description:
Today \`boundary_fns\` is consulted by:
- ir_reducer::try_reduce_call_with_descrs (via is_trivially_inlinable)
- ir_inline::inline_calls_once
- ir_inline::inline_tail_calls_once

After Phase E the latter two are gone. Verify only the reducer
consults boundary_fns. The typer-side narrowing decision (whether
to widen to declared spec) stays untouched — that's a separate
concern logged when RED.9 landed.

## Acceptance

- grep boundary_fns shows callers only in ir_reducer (and ir_lower
  for population) and a debug formatter or two.
- All tests + goldens green.

## Removes

The duplicated boundary checks (already gone after Phase E; this
ticket is the verification + comment cleanup).

comments.text: Closed not-actually-duplication. After .17 (block_callsites enumerator), the boundary check still lives in two sites because ir_inline and ir_reducer make DIFFERENT decisions about boundaries: ir_reducer has a trivially-inlinable exception (one-block, ≤1 stmt + Return); ir_inline never inlines a boundary. Centralizing would homogenize behavior we deliberately want differentiated. Not duplication of logic, just policy applied consistently at each decision site.

title: fz-CO.F.1 — Boundary uniformity: one consultation site
